gene_region.label: TLR10 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical TLR10 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup for ENSG00000174123 on the primary assembly, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: TLR10 encodes toll-like receptor 10, an innate immune receptor in the TLR1/6/10 cluster with immunomodulatory roles in microbial sensing, inflammation, and allergy/asthma association research.
gene_context.clinical_context: The bundled TLR10 knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation.
variant_effect_overview: TLR10 markers can tag innate immune signaling and the 4p14 TLR cluster rather than a single isolated receptor effect.
Interpretation should account for infection exposure, ancestry, immune-cell state, and linked TLR1/TLR6 variation.
condition_research_overview: Innate immune signaling and microbial exposure research
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: TLR10 methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene. Promoter-proximal or gene-body beta values may help frame accessibility or expression hypotheses, but they are not validated stand-alone biomarkers for asthma, eczema, allergy, immune activation, or treatment response.
methylation_effects: Promoter-proximal methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local regulatory state for TLR10 expression potential.
Interpret methylation with tissue, cell composition, environmental exposure, disease state, medication history, and genotype context.
methylation_condition_research: Asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, eosinophilic inflammation, and airway epithelial studies.
Immune-cell, epithelial-barrier, cytokine-signaling, and inflammatory-disease epigenetic research.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000174123: TLR10 coordinates and transcripts

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs4129009 is bundled as a TLR10 research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Research-level allergic and immune-inflammatory association marker; not a deterministic clinical allele.
functional_effects: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around TLR10.
Reported effects should be interpreted with ancestry, phenotype definition, tissue, and genotype dosage in mind.
associated_conditions: Innate immune signaling and microbial exposure research
research_context: Use this marker as a research context flag and not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Direction and effect size can vary across asthma, eczema, allergy, immune, epithelial, and ancestry-specific cohorts.
Confirm exact allele, build, zygosity, QC, and LD/haplotype context before drawing marker-level conclusions.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: TLR10 marker used for allergic-inflammation research context.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: TLR10 methylation can provide local regulatory context, but it does not establish the variant, haplotype, expression state, or phenotype direction for rs4129009.
